summary: This License, Development and Commercialization Agreement between Xencor, Inc. and Aimmune Therapeutics, Inc. grants Aimmune an exclusive, worldwide license to develop, manufacture, and commercialize Xencor's antibody (the "Product"). Xencor's key obligations include transferring necessary know-how and regulatory data, and refraining from competing with Aimmune on the Product. Aimmune is solely responsible for all development, manufacturing, and commercialization activities and associated costs, including obtaining and maintaining regulatory and pricing approvals. Aimmune must also provide Xencor with regular progress updates. Key risks include termination rights for either party in case of material breach, with a 60-day (30-day for non-payment) cure period, and Xencor's right to terminate for certain patent challenges by Aimmune. Xencor indemnifies Aimmune for losses arising from Xencor's breaches or specific product-related activities. Both parties are bound by confidentiality obligations for a specified period post-term.

summary: This Sponsorship Agreement, effective April 1, 2018, is between Jacksonville Jaguars, LLC (Club) and The ARC Group, Inc. (Sponsor), owner of Dick's Wings and Grill. Its purpose is to grant Sponsor various marketing and promotional rights related to the Jacksonville Jaguars NFL team. Key obligations for Sponsor include paying annual fees ranging from $200,000 to $216,490, providing annual food and beverage trade value ($35,000-$37,890), and additional payments for playoff games. In return, the Club grants rights such as stadium signage, radio and digital advertising, hospitality, and branded concession opportunities. The agreement runs until the later of the 2022/23 NFL season conclusion or February 2023. A notable risk for Sponsor is sole responsibility for all costs and expenses related to any product recalls, with the Club having the right to suspend benefits during such events.
termination_clause: This Agreement shall be binding on the parties hereto as of the Execution Date. The term of this Agreement (the "Term") shall commence as of April 1, 2018 (the " Effective Date") and shall expire upon the later of: (a) the conclusion of the 2022/23 NFL season and (b) the last day in February, 2023 (such expiration date, the " Scheduled Expiration Date "), unless sooner terminated pursuant to the terms of this Agreement.
liability_clause: Any costs or expenses incurred by Club or the Concessionaire with respect to any such recall shall be the sole responsibility of Sponsor.

summary: This Intellectual Property Agreement, between Armstrong Flooring, Inc. (Arizona) and AFI Licensing LLC, and AHF Holding, Inc. (Buyer) and Armstrong Hardwood Flooring Company (Company), facilitates the transfer and reciprocal licensing of intellectual property following a stock purchase. Arizona assigns specific IP (Copyrights, Patents, Trademarks, Know-How) to the Company. Both parties grant each other perpetual, non-exclusive, royalty-free licenses for their respective Copyrights, Know-How, and Patents in their designated business fields. Additionally, Arizona grants the Company limited-term licenses for specific trademarks (Arizona and Diamond Licensed Trademarks) with strict quality control and usage obligations. Key risks include termination for material breach, with partial termination possible for specific IP types. Each party indemnifies the other for breaches or gross negligence. The agreement disclaims implied warranties regarding IP validity or non-infringement, and includes a waiver of jury trial and provision for specific performance.

summary: This Global Maintenance Agreement between Azul Linhas Aéreas Brasileiras S/A (Company) and Avions de Transport Regional, G.I.E. (Repairer) establishes the terms for the Repairer to provide comprehensive maintenance services for Azul's ATR aircraft fleet and lease necessary stock. Key obligations include the Repairer performing services compliant with aviation regulations and the Company operating aircraft per technical documentation, providing activity reports, and making timely payments. The Company must also maintain extensive insurance coverage, naming the Repairer as an additional insured, and provide a security deposit. Notable risks include termination for insolvency or material default, with overdue payments incurring significant interest and recovery costs. The Repairer's liability is limited, excluding certain loss types, but not for death or personal injury due to negligence, and the Company indemnifies the Repairer against liabilities except for gross negligence. Confidentiality of shared information is also a critical aspect.

summary: This document comprises two amendments to existing Development and Commercial License Agreements between Cargill and BioAmber. The first amendment allows Cargill to deploy additional full-time equivalents for a work plan. The second, more substantial amendment, permits BioAmber to fund a Biotechnology Research Institute (BRI) project for succinic acid production using a re-engineered Methylotroph and to conduct a separate scale-up and production project. BioAmber's key obligations include ceasing funding for the BRI Project within 30 days of the Methylotroph achieving a specified succinic acid production concentration. Furthermore, BioAmber must convert its Pomacle and Sarnia succinic acid production facilities to exclusively utilize the CB1 biocatalyst, using best efforts to obtain regulatory approvals and scale up the CB1 strain. A notable risk is that unexpended BRI project funds must be reallocated to another project upon reaching the specified production milestone.
termination_clause: Notwithstanding the above, Bioamber may fund the BRI Project up until the Methylotroph (or re-engineered Methylotroph) demonstrates the ability to produce succinic acid (or salts thereof) from any feedstock at a concentration of [***] grams/liter succinic acid (or salts thereof). Within thirty (30) days of the Methylotroph (or re-engineered Methylotroph) demonstrating such production levels of succinic acid, Bioamber will cease any further funding and/or other support for the BRI Project. Further Bioamber will require that any unexpended funds received from Bioamber be utilized for a project other than the BRI Project.

summary: This Celebrity Endorsement Agreement between Bizzingo, Inc. and Joseph Theismann grants Bizzingo worldwide rights to use Theismann's name, likeness, and other "Property" for advertising and promoting its B2B interactive platform ("Network"). Theismann is obligated to provide promotional appearances and cooperate with Bizzingo's marketing efforts. In return, Bizzingo pays Theismann an initial bonus and royalties in common stock purchase warrants tied to the number of "Activated Users" on its Network. Key obligations include Bizzingo securing a $5M insurance policy for Theismann and Theismann warranting exclusive endorsement rights. Risks involve mutual indemnification for third-party claims: Bizzingo for Network-related issues, Theismann for breach of his warranties or infringement. Termination can occur with 30 days' notice for various breaches, including Bizzingo's insolvency or failure to pay royalties, or Theismann's misconduct or death. Theismann can also terminate at will. A separate confidentiality agreement is required.

summary: This Intellectual Property Agreement between Nuance Communications, Inc. and Cerence Inc. (SpinCo) establishes the framework for intellectual property rights following SpinCo's separation. Key obligations include Nuance licensing its IP to SpinCo for the SpinCo Field of Use, and SpinCo licensing its IP to Nuance for the Nuance Field of Use.
